steps: Triangle QRS has QR = x, RS = x, and SQ = y. The three sides sum to x + x + y = 2x + y.
The equation 2x + y = 37 equates the perimeter to 37. So 37 is the sum of the lengths of all three sides.
This matches the definition of perimeter: the total distance around the triangle.
choiceElimination:
• Choice A — 'The difference, in centimeters, between the lengths of sides QR and SQ': The equation adds all sides, not subtracts any two. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The difference, in centimeters, between the lengths of sides QR and RS': QR and RS are both x, so their difference is 0, not 37. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The sum of the lengths, in centimeters, of the three sides of the triangle': 2x + y = x + x + y = QR + RS + SQ = perimeter = 37. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The length, in centimeters, of one of the two sides of equal length': That would be x, not 37, which is the full sum. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
